claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you would like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wanted to check if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex, who is 3 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! A 3-year-old is within the eligible age range for Child Benefit.

Now, could you tell me about your living situation - do you live in the UK with Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK with Alex. We live together here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! That's helpful to know.

Just a couple more questions to confirm your eligibility:

Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a British citizen, do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or do you have another immigration status that gives you the right to live here?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen, so I have the right to live here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One more question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is your local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance (for example, if Alex were in foster care or any other care arrangement)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. Alex just lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

- ✅ **Age requirement met**: Alex is 3 years old, which is under 16
- ✅ **UK residency**: You live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✅ **Right to reside**: You're a British citizen
- ✅ **Responsibility for child**: Alex lives with you, so you're considered responsible for them
- ✅ **No competing claims**: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✅ **No council involvement**: The local council isn't paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. You'll need to apply through the official government channels.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old, which is under the age of 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK (Manchester), satisfying the residency requirement. Alex lives with the claimant, meaning the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with their 3-year-old child, Alex. They are responsible for Alex as the child lives with them. No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there is no local council involvement in supporting the child's accommodation or maintenance. All standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.

No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and the local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.

No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there is no local council involvement in supporting the child's accommodation or maintenance.
